title: France Proposes Tougher Hydrogen Fuel Mandate with Heavy Penalties for Non-Compliance by 2030
content: France is taking a harder line than Brussels on hydrogen fuel targets, launching a consultation to require renewable hydrogen and its derivatives to account for 1.5% of all transport fuels by 2030â€”half a percentage point higher than the EUâ€™s 1% under RED III. The French ecological transition ministry outlined a gradual ramp-up from 0.1% in 2026 to 2% by 2035, replacing the existing TIRUERT tax incentive system with the new Incentive for the Reduction of Carbon Intensity of Fuels (IRICC) framework. Fuel suppliers missing the targets would face fines of â‚¬80 [$86.50] per gigajoule of shortfall, plus â‚¬700 [$757] per tonne of CO2 not avoided, setting some of the strictest enforcement mechanisms in Europe.
author: Analysts Account
